title: Introduction to Informed Search
content:
Introduction to Informed SearchUniformed Search vs Informed Search
Uniformed Search (aka blind search)

Informed Search (aka heuristic search)

Search guided by heuristic evaluation of how promising a node is.






Uniformed SearchDijkstra’s Algorithm. It works by visiting vertices in the graph starting with the object’s starting point. Then repeatedly examinges the closest not-yet examinged vertex, adding its vertices to the set of vertices to be examined. As long as none of the edges have a negative cost, Dijkstra’s Algorithm is guaranteed to find a shortest path from the starting point to the goal.

Greedy SearchStrategy: Expand node in frontier with smallest heuristic value.

Heuristic, some estimate of how far from the goal any vertex is. 

Property:
Instead of selecting the vertex closest to the starting point, it selects the vertex closest to the goal.
Not guaranteed to find a shortest path.
Runs much quicker than Dijkstra’s Algorithm because it uses the heuristic function to guide its way towards the goal very quickly.



In this example, the goal (blue point) is to the south of the starting position (pink one),  Greedy Search will tend to focus on paths that lead southwards. Yellow represents those nodes with a high heuristic value (high cost to get to the goal) and black represents nodes with a low heuristic value (low cost to get to the goal).

ProblemWhen there exists obstacles. Dijkstra’s Algorithm is garanteed to find a shortest path but with a lot work to do.

Greedy search does less work but the path it finds is clearly not as good.

The trouble is that Greedy Best-First-Search is “greedy” and tries to move towards the goal even if it’s not the right path. Since it only considers the cost to get to the goal and ignores the cost of the path so far, it keeps going even if the path it’s on has become really long.
The A* AlgorithmA* is like Dijkstra’s Algorithm in that it can be used to find a shortest path. A* is like Greedy Best-First-Search in that it can use a heuristic to guide itself. In the simple case, it is as fast as Greedy Best-First-Search:



Uniform-cost orders by path cost, or backward cost 
Greedy orders by goal proximity, or forward cost 
A* Search orders by the sum: 

When should A* terminate?Only stops when we dequeue a goal.


Admissible Heuristics

Properties
Bottom of the lattice is the zero heuristic.

Top of the lattice is the exact heuristic.


In this example, the path that A* graph search consider is : S, SA, SC, SAC, SCG. In the graph search process, G (5+0) is not considered.

title: The Ising Model (伊辛模型)
content:
概述
Ising Model来自热力学系统，是统计力学中铁磁性的数学模型。该模型由离散变量组成，表示原子自选的磁偶极矩 (magnetic dipole moments)。二维方格Ising Mode是显示相变最简单的统计模型之一。

定义Ising 模型是一种数学模型，与实际的物理系统不对应。这是一个巨大的（方形）站点网格，每个站点都可以处于两种状态之一。我们用索引标记每个站点, 我们将两个状态称作 和。 要说第个站点处于状态，我们写作。

Ising Model作为数学模型较为抽象，我们可以用以下一些物理系统来代表。

一块磁铁。每个位点代表材料中一个特定的自旋，每个自旋像是一个迷你磁铁，拥有自己的迷你磁矩。如果所有自旋都是对齐的，那么整个自旋晶体就像一个有着宏观磁矩的大磁铁。

一种合金。每个位点代表一个晶格中的一个原子。如黄铜中代表该位点是铜原子，表示该位点是锌原子。


Hamiltonian of the Ising Model (哈密顿量)
该公式可以分为两部分：
： 相邻自旋的相互作用项。
​    的大小：相邻自旋耦合强度，即想要/反对齐的程度。
​    的符号：邻居是喜欢对齐还是反对对齐。
： 外场项，外磁场可分为自旋向上/向下，因此一个能量较高而另一个较低。
​    的大小：场的强度，告诉一个旋转的能量比另一个高了多少。
​    的符号：首选是向上旋转还是向下旋转

因为每个单独的自旋都会收到外部场影响，需要对所有位点求和找到整体对能量的总贡献。
而自旋-自旋相互作用只发生在两个自旋之间，必须对成对的位点求和找到与能力总相互作用。

Reference:https://stanford.edu/~jeffjar/statmech/intro4.html

title: Welcome to Hexo!
content:
This is my very first post. 
Check documentation for more info.
Quick StartCreate a new post$ hexo new "My New Post"

More info: Writing
Run server$ hexo server

More info: Server
Generate static files$ hexo generate

More info: Generating
Deploy to remote sites$ hexo clean && hexo d -g

More info: Deployment
Theme Settings
I use hex-theme-keep in this blog.
More info: https://keep-docs.xpoet.cn/usage-tutorial/configuration-guide.html
